question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly poll getLatestNetworkStake() to observe the exact stakingRewardRate and epochDay fields in NetworkStake, then time their own staking transactions on-chain to front-run the reward period boundary and claim a disproportionately large pending reward before other stakers, causing theft of unpaid staking rewards from the shared reward pool?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getLatestNetworkStake() returns the single latest NetworkStake row (via networkStakeRepository.findLatest()) without any freshness or staleness check, causing the returned stakingRewardRate to reflect a stale epoch, and allowing the attacker to make staking decisions based on outdated reward rates that differ from the actual on-chain state, leading to theft of unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the unreservedStakingRewardBalance field returned by getLatestNetworkStake() to determine when the reward pool is near-empty, then time a large staking transaction to claim the last available rewards before other accounts, effectively stealing unpaid staking rewards from legitimate stakers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror-restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user use the maxStakingRewardRatePerHbar and stakeTotal fields from getLatestNetworkStake() to calculate the exact reward rate per node, identify the highest-rewarding node, and redirect their stake to that node just before the staking period ends, stealing a larger share of unpaid rewards from accounts already staked to that node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodeRewardFeeNumerator and nodeRewardFeeDenominator fields exposed by getLatestNetworkStake() to calculate the exact node operator fee fraction, then identify nodes where the fee fraction is lowest and stake to those nodes to maximize their own pending reward at the expense of other stakers, constituting theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the reservedStakingRewards field from getLatestNetworkStake() to determine the exact amount of HBAR reserved for staking payouts, then use this information to calculate the maximum claimable reward and craft on-chain transactions to claim the maximum share before the reservation is exhausted, stealing unpaid rewards from other stakers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the rewardBalanceThreshold field in the NetworkStake response to determine when the staking reward account (0.0.800) balance is near the threshold, then time a large stake to claim rewards just before the threshold is crossed and rewards are reduced, stealing unpaid staking rewards from other participants?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user use the maxStakeRewarded field from getLatestNetworkStake() to determine the maximum stake eligible for rewards per node, then split their stake across multiple accounts to stay just under the per-account cap and maximize total reward extraction, stealing unpaid staking rewards that would otherwise be distributed to other stakers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stakingPeriodsStored field from getLatestNetworkStake() to determine the exact 365-day window for reward accumulation, then craft a staking strategy that maximizes accumulated pendingReward across the maximum number of stored periods, stealing unpaid rewards that should have been forfeited after the window expires?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the stakingPeriodDuration field from getLatestNetworkStake() to determine the exact length of each staking period in nanoseconds, then time their staking transactions to land at the very start of a new period and accumulate a full period's reward with minimal stake duration, stealing unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxTotalReward field from getLatestNetworkStake() to determine the total reward cap for the current period, then calculate the exact stake needed to claim the maximum share of the reward pool before other stakers, effectively stealing unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stakingStartThreshold field from getLatestNetworkStake() to determine the minimum total stake required for rewards to be distributed, then monitor when total stake approaches this threshold and time their staking to be included in the first rewarded period, stealing unpaid staking rewards from accounts that staked before the threshold was met?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stakingRewardFeeFraction (derived from stakingRewardFeeNumerator/stakingRewardFeeDenominator) exposed by getLatestNetworkStake() to calculate the exact fee taken from staking rewards, then identify periods where the fee fraction is lowest and maximize their reward extraction, stealing unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensusTimestamp field in the NetworkStake response to determine the exact time of the last staking period update, calculate the time until the next period boundary, and time their staking transactions to maximize pending reward accumulation, stealing unpaid staking rewards from other stakers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the epochDay field from getLatestNetworkStake() to determine the current staking epoch, then use this to calculate exactly when the 365-day reward forfeiture window applies to other accounts, and stake to nodes that are about to have their forfeited rewards redistributed, stealing those unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSupply(Bound)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a timestamp Bound with a maxTimestamp just before a known staking reward distribution event, causing getSupply() to call accountBalanceRepository.getSupplyHistory() with a window that excludes the reward payout, returning an artificially low unreleased supply figure that misrepresents the available reward pool and enables theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSupply(Bound)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp Bound where getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() returns 0 (lower is null) and adjustUpperBound() returns Long.MAX_VALUE (upper is null), causing getSupply() to query the entire account_balance history and return a supply figure that includes staking reward account balances not yet distributed, enabling the attacker to calculate and steal unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSupply(Bound)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the optimalLowerBound calculation in getSupply() — where minTimestamp is set to Math.max(minTimestamp, getFirstDayOfMonth(maxTimestamp, -1)) — by supplying a maxTimestamp at the very start of a month, causing optimalLowerBound to jump back a full month and include staking reward balance snapshots from a prior period, inflating the unreleased supply figure and enabling theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSupply(Bound)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a timestamp Bound where minTimestamp equals maxTimestamp (a point-in-time query), causing getSupply() to call accountBalanceRepository.getSupplyHistory() with a zero-width window that returns the balance snapshot at exactly the moment a staking reward was paid out, revealing the exact amount of unpaid rewards and enabling their theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSupply(Bound)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getSupply() does not validate whether the provided timestamp falls within a valid staking period, allowing them to query supply at arbitrary historical timestamps to reconstruct the staking reward distribution history and identify periods where unpaid rewards were not claimed, enabling targeted theft of those rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSupply(Bound)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on getSupply() to perform high-frequency timestamp scanning — iterating through nanosecond-precision timestamps — to reconstruct the exact balance of staking reward account 0.0.800 at each staking period boundary, enabling precise calculation of unclaimed pending rewards and their theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSupply(Bound)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a timestamp Bound using the GT operator (which causes getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() to increment the value by 1) to shift the query window by exactly one nanosecond, bypassing a staking reward snapshot boundary and causing getSupply() to return a supply figure that excludes a reward distribution event, enabling theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSupply(Bound)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LT operator in Bound.adjustUpperBound() — which decrements the upper bound by 1 — to craft a timestamp range that ends exactly one nanosecond before a staking reward payout, causing getSupply() to return a pre-payout supply figure that reveals the exact reward amount about to be distributed, enabling theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getFirstDayOfMonth(long, int)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a nanosecond timestamp near Long.MAX_VALUE to getSupply(), causing getFirstDayOfMonth() to compute firstDay.toLocalDate().atStartOfDay(ZoneOffset.UTC).toEpochSecond() * DomainUtils.NANOS_PER_SECOND to overflow, producing a negative optimalLowerBound that is then used as Math.max(minTimestamp, optimalLowerBound), effectively bypassing the lower bound restriction and querying the full staking reward history to steal unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getFirstDayOfMonth(long, int)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp of 0 (epoch) to getSupply(), causing getFirstDayOfMonth() to compute a date in 1970 with monthOffset=-1 (November 1969), producing a negative epoch second that when multiplied by NANOS_PER_SECOND yields a large negative long, which Math.max() then ignores in favor of minTimestamp=0, causing the query to scan the entire account_balance history and expose staking reward data enabling theft of unpaid rewards?
